title: Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Nagaland

overview.short: Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Nagaland, India.
overview.description: Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Nagaland, India. It is situated close to the India-Myanmar border in Pungro circle of Kiphire district, and covers an area of 642 ha (1,590 acres). Fakim village is the gateway to the sanctuary. It was established in 1983.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (119 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (123 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
